title: The Power of Active Listening
education_text: Active listening means fully concentrating on what your partner is saying rather than planning your response. In ENM relationships, this skill becomes even more critical.

prompt_text: What is one thing your partner does that makes you feel truly heard?

title: Check-In Rituals
prompt_text: What would an ideal weekly check-in look like for us?
education_text: Regular check-ins help couples stay connected. Many ENM couples find that scheduled conversations reduce anxiety.

title: Understanding Boundaries vs. Rules
education_text: Boundaries are about what you will do. Rules are about what you want others to do. Understanding this difference is foundational to ethical non-monogamy.

prompt_text: Name one boundary that feels non-negotiable for you right now.

title: Boundary Evolution
prompt_text: How do you want to handle it when a boundary needs to change?
education_text: Boundaries are not static. As relationships grow, what feels safe may shift. Having a process for renegotiation builds trust.

title: Jealousy as Information
education_text: Jealousy is not a sign of failure. It is an emotional signal pointing to unmet needs, fears, or insecurities worth exploring.

prompt_text: What emotion comes up first when you imagine your partner with someone else?

title: Compersion
prompt_text: Have you ever felt happy about a partner's joy with someone else? What was that like?
education_text: Compersion — sometimes called the opposite of jealousy — is the feeling of joy when a partner experiences happiness with another person.

title: ENM Styles Overview
education_text: Ethical non-monogamy includes many styles: polyamory, open relationships, swinging, relationship anarchy, and more. There is no single right way.

prompt_text: Which relationship structure appeals to you most right now, and why?

title: Hierarchy in Relationships
prompt_text: How do you feel about the idea of primary and secondary partners?
education_text: Hierarchical polyamory ranks relationships by priority. Non-hierarchical approaches treat each connection as unique. Neither is inherently better.

title: Safer Sex Agreements
education_text: Safer sex agreements are living documents. They outline testing schedules, barrier use, and disclosure expectations with all partners.

prompt_text: What does sexual safety mean to you in practice?

title: Desire and New Connections
prompt_text: How do you think new attraction would affect our intimate life together?
education_text: New Relationship Energy (NRE) can feel intoxicating. Understanding its temporary nature helps couples navigate it without destabilizing existing bonds.

title: Time Management in ENM
education_text: One of the most practical challenges of non-monogamy is time. Calendar sharing, date scheduling, and protecting couple time are essential skills.

prompt_text: How would you want to handle scheduling if one of us starts dating someone new?

title: Disclosure Agreements
prompt_text: What do you want to know about your partner's other connections, and what would you rather not know?
education_text: Disclosure preferences vary widely. Some people want full details, others prefer a dont-ask-dont-tell approach. Most healthy ENM relationships land somewhere in between.
